repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: Support Proposition 1 null-annihilator criterion for proximal ATE point identification

published_prior_art.cite: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 and BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023: linear functionals can be identified/root-n estimable despite nonunique or weakly identified bridge functions

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1 is static bridge-envelope/selector taxonomy, not a local-to-incomplete rho_n phase diagram

published_prior_art.cite: BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023 and SmuclerRobinsRotnitzky2025 are closest but do not give the proposed three-region rho_n proximal ATE phase diagram
SmuclerRobinsRotnitzky2025 gives the closest uniform-confidence-set lower bound and binary special case but not this rho_n-indexed projection set

structure_flags.label: Introduction
structure_flags.one_line: The introduction has five labelled subparagraphs and merges the required 'how research benefits from the conclusion' item into implications instead of giving the sixth required subparagraph.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The finite row-space/null-annihilator frontier duplicates eid_proximal_phase_v1 Support Proposition 1; it is acceptable as scaffold but not novel.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal misses Bennett et al. (COLT 2023) on strongly identified functionals of weakly identified functions and Smucler-Robins-Rotnitzky (Biometrika 2025) on confidence sets for linear functionals of integral-equation solutions, both closer than several listed inverse-problem comparators.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The abstract promises to turn CanaySantosShaikh2013's non-testability warning into an operational rule, but no section 8 statement is phrased as a strict tightening or equivalence against that non-testability result.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The critical value c_{1-alpha} is only described as an 'appropriate chi-square or Gaussian-projection critical value'; the rank, nuisance, and weak/null-space cases determining it are not pinned.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add the missing sixth introduction subparagraph and add Bennett et al. (2023) plus Smucler-Robins-Rotnitzky (2025) to related work with location-level anchors.

Rewrite Conjecture 2 explicitly relative to Smucler-Robins-Rotnitzky: state whether the residual projection set avoids, specializes, or contradicts their score-inversion failure and define the exact critical value rule.

Strengthen Conjecture 1 into a finite-dimensional Gaussian experiment frontier with an explicit lower bound/equivalence theorem showing rho_n is necessary and sufficient, not just a natural concentration parameter.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature positioning and Conjecture 2 critical-value definition before sending this to Stage 0.
